modules.name: modulus-assistant
modules.description: Unified everyday assistant: calendar, tasks, reminders, weather, briefings, and day-planning in one module.

modules.name: modulus-browser
modules.description: Lets agents drive a real headless browser (navigate, read, click, type, screenshot) with SSRF guards. Heavy: needs Playwright + Chromium; intended for Standard/Heavy tiers, never a Raspberry Pi.

modules.name: modulus-codex
modules.description: Escalate hard tasks from the local Qwen models to OpenAI Codex over a ChatGPT subscription (OAuth). Codex acts as Modulus's deep-reasoning brain for coding, reasoning, writing, planning, and analysis the small models can't do at quality. Local-first.

modules.name: modulus-discord
modules.description: Discord as a second chat surface alongside Telegram. One Modulus process, two surfaces — same model, memory, extensions, and confirm-tier UX. Gateway WebSocket (no inbound webhook), DM-allowlist or per-channel opt-in, button-based confirm-tier prompts.

modules.name: modulus-minimax
modules.description: Adds MiniMax (chatcompletion_v2) as an LLM provider. Not OpenAI-compatible.

modules.name: modulus-openai
modules.description: Register OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoints as Modulus LLM provider aliases.

modules.name: modulus-voice
modules.description: Two-way voice for Telegram: outbound replies via Piper TTS, inbound voice-note transcription via whisper.cpp. /voice on enables voice replies; /voice transcribe on enables voice-in.

modules.name: modulus-websearch
modules.description: Web search as a safe, read-only LLM tool (DuckDuckGo or a self-hosted SearXNG). Results are SSRF-guarded, stripped to plain text, and framed as untrusted reference data.
